design_philosophy.core_loop: Enter races to earn prize money → purchase vehicle upgrades → advance to higher-tier races with larger entry fees and greater rewards
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Risk-versus-reward between upgrading your vehicle now versus saving capital to advance to more lucrative race tiers with an under-equipped car
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Build and customize a competitive racing machine while managing financial resources across a career progression
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Forces players into a recurring financial decision structure rather than purely skill-based progression; the mandatory split-screen view even in single-player emphasizes competitive racing over individual mastery

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Players control difficulty indirectly through vehicle upgrade timing and tier advancement choices; can choose to over-prepare before advancing tiers or take risks with under-equipped vehicles

progression.upgrade_system: Engine, tires, shocks, and armor upgrades purchased with race winnings; limited-use offensive items (oil slicks, mines) available for tactical deployment
progression.unlock_mechanism: Higher race tiers unlock by paying mandatory entry fees; progression gated by financial accumulation rather than skill gates
replayability_hooks: Track editor allowing custom course creation and design
Three vehicle types with cosmetic differentiation
Career mode structured around tier advancement

world_building_techniques: Career progression structure implies a racing league/circuit with escalating stakes

danger_signals: Competitive multiplayer structure; resource scarcity (prize money) creates rivalry incentives
environmental_storytelling: Track design complexity (ovals vs. complex hills/curves) suggests escalating difficulty through race circuits
